// Fixture for cmd/check-sql tests. NOT a real .go file (the .txt
// extension keeps the toolchain from compiling or formatting it). The
// test renames a temp copy to .go and points check-sql at the temp
// tree.
package violations

import (
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"strings"
)

// fakeBuilder mimics chsql.Builder's WriteString / WriteSQL surface
// so the AST walker has a Sel target that resembles the real one.
type fakeBuilder struct{ sb strings.Builder }

func (b *fakeBuilder) WriteString(s string) { b.sb.WriteString(s) }
func (b *fakeBuilder) WriteSQL(s string)    { b.sb.WriteString(s) }

func sprintfHit() string {
	return fmt.Sprintf("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = %d", 1)
}

func fprintfHit(w io.Writer) {
	fmt.Fprintf(w, "INSERT INTO t VALUES (%d)", 1)
}

func writeStringHit() string {
	b := &fakeBuilder{}
	b.WriteString("SELECT ")
	b.WriteString(" FROM t")
	b.WriteString(" WHERE x = 1")
	b.WriteString(" GROUP BY g")
	b.WriteString(" ORDER BY t")
	b.WriteString(" LIMIT 10")
	b.WriteString(" PREWHERE p")
	b.WriteString(" INNER JOIN r ON x = y")
	return b.sb.String()
}

func writeSQLHit() string {
	b := &fakeBuilder{}
	b.WriteSQL(" UNION ALL")
	b.WriteSQL(" WITH RECURSIVE c AS (SELECT 1)")
	return b.sb.String()
}

func legitTokenWrites() string {
	b := &fakeBuilder{}
	// operator-glue tokens — must NOT trip the rule
	b.WriteSQL(" = ")
	b.WriteSQL(" AND ")
	b.WriteString(", ")
	b.WriteString("(")
	return b.sb.String()
}

func legitSprintf() string {
	// no SQL clause keyword — must NOT trip
	return fmt.Sprintf("hello %s", "world")
}
